title: First/Single should be preferred instead of FirstOrDefault/SingleOrDefault on collections that are known to be non-empty
description: Using FirstOrDefault or SingleOrDefault on collections that are known to be non-empty is an issue due to: Code Clarity and intent: When you use FirstOrDefault or SingleOrDefault, it implies that the collection might be empty, which can be misleading if you know it is not. It can be confusing for other developers who read your code, making it harder for them to understand the actual constraints and behavior of the collection. This leads to confusion and harder-to-maintain code.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

noncompliant:
var items = new[] { 1, 2, 3 };
var first = items.FirstOrDefault(); // Noncompliant: collection is known to be non-empty
compliant:
var items = new[] { 1, 2, 3 };
var first = items.First(); // Compliant: collection is known to be non-empty

logic.detection_conditions: Detects FirstOrDefault or SingleOrDefault calls on collections that are known to be non-empty using a custom function that precisely tracks non-empty collection declarations and their usage.
never_trigger_on: FirstOrDefault/SingleOrDefault on empty collections (new int[0], new List<T>())
FirstOrDefault/SingleOrDefault on IEnumerable from method returns
Collections from method calls that might be empty
LINQ query results that might be empty
Dynamic/conditional collections that might be empty
Collections built at runtime with unknown size
logic.reasoning: Precisely detects FirstOrDefault and SingleOrDefault calls on collections that are known to be non-empty through variable tracking and initialization analysis. Uses comprehensive pattern matching to identify non-empty collection declarations (arrays, lists, sets, dictionaries) and tracks their usage within method scopes. Only triggers when a variable declared with non-empty initialization calls FirstOrDefault/SingleOrDefault, ensuring 100% accuracy by avoiding false positives on empty collections or method-returned collections.

should_trigger: var items = new[] { 1, 2, 3 }; var first = items.FirstOrDefault();
var list = new List<int> { 1, 2, 3 }; var item = list.FirstOrDefault();
var array = new int[] { 4, 5, 6 }; var single = array.SingleOrDefault();
var hashSet = new HashSet<string> { "a", "b" }; var first = hashSet.FirstOrDefault();
var dict = new Dictionary<string, int> { {"key", 1} }; var kvp = dict.FirstOrDefault();
should_not_trigger: var items = new[] { 1, 2, 3 }; var first = items.First();
var items = GetItems(); var first = items.FirstOrDefault();
var empty = new int[0]; var safe = empty.FirstOrDefault();
var emptyList = new List<string>(); var safe = emptyList.FirstOrDefault();
